name: Donna Edwards
subject: 100 days left - can I count on you?
body:
Together, we can change Prince George's County.
|
---
[ ](http://action.donnaforprincegeorges.org/page/m/74feff72/767134fb/5cbab275/43382a10/2019607658/VEsH/ "http://www.donnaforprincegeorges.org/")

| | Hi Friend, First, I want to thank you for your support over the last several months of this campaign. I couldn't do any of this without you, and I am grateful for the people who believe in my vision for Prince George's County--one where the government is accountable to the needs of all its residents and there is a strong focus on our school system producing a workforce that is ready to succeed in a 21st century economy. We are only 100 days until the June 26th primary, and we need your help now more than ever. At the beginning of my run for County Executive, I made a promise that my campaign would be funded by grassroots supporters like you, not rich developers that don't have Prince George's County residents' best interests at heart.[ **I want to end corruption and the influence of big business in our county, and I can 't do that without you. Will you give $10 or more to my campaign today?**](http://action.donnaforprincegeorges.org/page/m/74feff72/767134fb/5cbab275/43382a11/2019607658/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dfe-website?refcode=website") I want to reiterate that this election is about the future of Prince George's County. We've made great strides but stand at a critical crossroads that will determine success for our children, for our families, and for our economy in the years ahead. Now, with 100 days left before the primary election, I am asking for your support so that together, we can bring real change to Prince George's County. [**I cannot do this without you - I hope you will donate $10 or more today.**](http://action.donnaforprincegeorges.org/page/m/74feff72/767134fb/5cbab275/43382a11/2019607658/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dfe-website?refcode=website") Developers don't determine the future of Prince George's County - you do.[**I hope you will join me and help me reach the finish line as we approach the primary election. **](http://action.donnaforprincegeorges.org/page/m/74feff72/767134fb/5cbab275/43382a11/2019607658/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dfe-website?refcode=website") Thank you again for all your support, and for all the work you do. -Donna

subject: 100 days.

#
Friends,
Today marks 100 days out from primary day in Maryland, and we mustn't forget that our collective successes up until this point are a testament to our courage, shared vision for a brighter future, and willingness to come together for a stronger, more inclusive state.
Since announcing our campaign, we've traveled across Maryland meeting with our neighbors to hear their concerns and learn about the direction they'd like to see our state move in.
One thing is clear: **Maryland is ready to dream again.**
**[Help us move this state forward and bring progressive politics back to Maryland. Donate $5 today!](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a766/7ab0e2c4/7f55d9d0/349a7ce6/44803365/VEsH/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiT0hRMDMxODhBIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bj-st3-18?refcode=OHQ03188A")**
We must move to place a highly qualified teacher in every classroom, ensure every Marylander has access to quality and affordable healthcare, fight climate change, increase the minimum wage to $15 per hour, create jobs in our urban and rural communities and make every community safe from the ravages of violent crime, and finally treat drug addiction as a public health crisis
We can no longer afford half measures and complicit silence from our governor, not when an extremist right-wing federal government threatens our values and all the progress we have made together.
**[Work with me to help usher in a brighter future for Maryland, today.](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a766/7ab0e2c4/7f55d9d0/349a7ce6/44803365/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiT0hRMDMxODhBIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bj-st3-18?refcode=OHQ03188A")**
Onward,
Ben

name: The White House
subject: What’s troubling New Hampshire
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=159c0f19b5ff8adfecb0489cea6c5e32e5dc8d7931bdce8f6f8438801da216184645802fbeec0ad0289274217b67d1da91a4bad7878070e2

What’s troubling New Hampshire

New Hampshire has had the second highest rate of opioid-involved overdose deaths in the Nation since 2014. A poll conducted last year found that drugs were the biggest problem facing the Granite State—the first time in the poll’s history that a majority named any single issue as the most important.

Today, President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump travel to Manchester, New Hampshire. The President will address America’s opioid epidemic at Manchester Community College and discuss his Administration’s initiative to stop opioid abuse and reduce drug supply and demand, which has four components:

1. Address the driving forces of the opioid crisis, including over-prescription, illicit drug supplies, and insufficient access to evidence-based treatment.

2. Reduce demand and over-prescription in part by educating Americans about the dangers of opioid and other drug use.

3. Cut off the supply of illicit drugs by cracking down on international and domestic supply chains that devastate American communities.

4. Help those struggling with addiction through evidence-based treatment and recovery support services.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=159c0f19b5ff8adf65d4df9941bd2df8b9ef702983f4463f9a907dcb4fabc7bf250b690480fb2deaef4eb7bbe5f2002dab840218d6793a12
Watch live as the President addresses the opioid crisis at 2:40 p.m. ET today.

Learn more:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=159c0f19b5ff8adf3093f0a59873090ab4935b7ea37476ff58a74bf9887c2ff237cd04cf82adf3ce46c9b31584e3cc6ba8aa0a558fe14915
How America will win the war on opioid addiction

New frontiers

Last week, President Trump took time during a cross-country trip to the U.S.-Mexico border to address military service members at the Marine Corps Air Station Miramar in San Diego, California.

The President touched on many themes, including thanking our hardworking military men and women for their service and dedication to the cause. He also charted a path forward, echoing themes from his National Space Council—a group chaired by Vice President Mike Pence that the President resurrected after a 24-year hiatus.

“From the very beginning, many of our astronauts have been soldiers, sailors, airmen, Coast Guardsmen and Marines,” the President said. “We're going to lead the way in space.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=159c0f19b5ff8adfa5d317a7b0acc7e2c9931843c392ba465fc71a7a5523184f08b9a47c24aa739bb1a18345ec6b7bc795669c2b0937bced
Watch President Trump’s speech to service members at Miramar.

Infrastructure: The Federal role

The Federal Government provides important infrastructure funding, but it often does so inefficiently and with numerous restrictions. Washington depends on taxpayers at the state and local level, yet Federal funding is too often directed by priorities of those inside the Beltway.

President Trump proposes to fix this problem by maintaining the traditional balance of Federal and non-Federal funding, while investing in a way that stretches every dollar and delivers on local needs. His proposal will keep existing Federal funding in place, including for the Highway Trust Fund.

The President’s plan would improve on existing policy by directing half of Federal funds to an Incentives Program for State and local applicants. This system keeps infrastructure decisions out of the D.C. bureaucracy and in State and local hands.

Learn more:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=159c0f19b5ff8adf6660a98599ceaa1ca55bd710d82be669b61c417fe85424a1adc3a153cb1522f7ac1c4de28cd0519d969fb5c65e593a03
What you need to know about infrastructure funding

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump and Prime Minister Leo Varadkar of Ireland | March 15, 2018 (Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead)

POTUS TODAY

This morning, after receiving his daily intelligence briefing, President Trump will travel to Manchester Community College to deliver remarks on combatting the opioid crisis.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=159c0f19b5ff8adf8a1d9537faf3e84e8df3260b4c1b8d0378b9f5b3dde97e86acbec5484ff0508e31223c040874ce33dece012a223fecdd
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=159c0f19b5ff8adfecdaa6b13732236d09f6f1f7e0f5ba644789b6cbca3fe60053c7bf50ce8610dfd284e5a7bb7c3bfb74c968253f7e2aa4
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d48e983031b5d31087110f2b9a7f853fa63a6a5bd690ef944b1afef6ab906fb69f9d2a168a7a4235c374f30087012cd74c165283252d0adc7a
Unsubscribe

name: Tim Kaine
subject: Unfinished business
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
[Like anyone in my position, there is one question I have to answer: Why are you running?](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=KIjXRyo2T1SNrnklmHQq268b%2FPt17rW%2B)

And the answer I keep coming back to is that I have some unfinished business to settle.

When I finished my term as Governor in 2010, I had no intention of running for another office. 16 years of service at the state and local level was a real honor but there were other challenges out there for me. Then a Senate seat surprisingly opened up in 2011. And I asked myself - _do I have any unfinished business?_

And I did. Despite my efforts campaigning against it, voters passed an anti-LGBT amendment to the Virginia Constitution in 2006. That was a real disappointment. And I had been working with Virginia Indian tribes for years to get them the federal recognition they deserved. I had testified on their behalf and helped our bipartisan Congressional delegation advocate for them. But we kept falling short.

So when I declared for Senate in 2011, I wanted to help make progress in these areas beyond what I could do in local or state office. _And we have._

I signed on to a Senate amicus brief advocating marriage equality for all and celebrated when it became law of the land in 2013. And I stood on the floor of the Senate with my colleague Mark Warner in January of this year when our tribal recognition bill finally passed.

While there is still much work to do on both these issues, on an elemental level I have been able to see two things that were important to Virginians completed. [But as my first term closes out and I ask myself again if I have any unfinished business left in my political service, the answer is: yes, now there's even more.](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=Co13Kij0dtmasbgQUTO4DrVSqGw1G1Cu)

I want to make meaningful progress to protect our kids and communities from gun violence. I want to finally do comprehensive reform of our immigration laws so that we can continue to attract talented people from around the globe. I want to make sure that we offer students affordable college but also meaningful technical and apprenticeship opportunities. I want to keep reducing unemployment among veterans and military spouses. I want to drive prescription drug costs down and give folks more options for health coverage. _And there 's so much more._

You have taught me an important lesson. If you want to help people and you take the time to listen to them, there will ALWAYS be unfinished business on your "To Do" list. I have more to do and I would be honored to have your support as I keep at the work.

_Tim_

name: Barbara Lee
subject: I boycotted Trump's inauguration. Here's why:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, www
body:
The best way to hold Donald Trump and the GOP accountable is to elect more Democrats who will take them on.
---
When Donald Trump was sworn in as president, I made the decision to boycott his inauguration. I could not in good conscience celebrate an incoming president who had normalized the most extreme fringes of the Republican Party.

After riding racism and fear to the White House, Trump is now pushing policies that harm people of color, immigrants, and working people. For many of us, his presidency represents an attack on our very existence.

But we cannot afford to give up hope. There is far too much at stake for us to retreat from this fight.

November's election is our opportunity to show that the American people reject this administration's destructive agenda. That's something we can only do together -- and only if we start right now.

**Contribute $3 today if you're ready to elect Democrats who will take on Trump and defend our communities. We can't do it without you.**

** Unfortunately, the rising tide of hate is a reality we must continue to grapple with -- even at the highest levels of our government.

Let's not forget that Donald Trump led the birther movement to question the legitimacy of Barack Obama -- our first African American President. Instead of rejecting Trump's racist attacks, the Republican Party threw their support behind him.

Trump also sent a clear signal to white supremacists that it was time to "take their country back."

We will not go back to the days where I could not enroll in a public school unless it was segregated.

The days when my dad, a World War II and Korean War veteran, was denied entry at restaurants due to the color of his skin.

The days when my mother was refused the opportunity to buy a house because African Americans weren't allowed to purchase homes.

We must do everything we can to fight against the forces of hatred that are on the rise under Trump -- and that starts with organizing in our communities ahead of November's election.

If you like staying in touch, but want to receive only the most important messages, click [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/405226ce/4067b0a/512337eea/7e48d468/6702930/VEsFBg/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/signup/less-email?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=33+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180319_bl_nd_b&source=em_20180319_bl_nd_b&refcode=em_20180319_bl_nd_b"). This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. Thanks for supporting our party.
clean_body: The best way to hold Donald Trump and the GOP accountable is to elect more Democrats who will take them on. When Donald Trump was sworn in as president, I made the decision to boycott his inauguration. I could not in good conscience celebrate an incoming president who had normalized the most extreme fringes of the Republican Party. After riding racism and fear to the White House, Trump is now pushing policies that harm people of color, immigrants, and working people. For many of us, his presidency represents an attack on our very existence. But we cannot afford to give up hope. There is far too much at stake for us to retreat from this fight. November's election is our opportunity to show that the American people reject this administration's destructive agenda. That's something we can only do together -- and only if we start right now. Contribute $3 today if you're ready to elect Democrats who will take on Trump and defend our communities. We can't do it without you. "" "" "" "" "" "" Unfortunately, the rising tide of hate is a reality we must continue to grapple with -- even at the highest levels of our government. Let's not forget that Donald Trump led the birther movement to question the legitimacy of Barack Obama -- our first African American President. Instead of rejecting Trump's racist attacks, the Republican Party threw their support behind him. Trump also sent a clear signal to white supremacists that it was time to "take their country back." We will not go back to the days where I could not enroll in a public school unless it was segregated. The days when my dad, a World War II and Korean War veteran, was denied entry at restaurants due to the color of his skin. The days when my mother was refused the opportunity to buy a house because African Americans weren't allowed to purchase homes. We must do everything we can to fight against the forces of hatred that are on the rise under Trump -- and that starts with organizing in our communities ahead of November's election. "" Thanks for being part of the resistance. Barbara Lee U.S. Representative, California Contributions or gifts to the Democratic National Committee are not tax deductible. | | No American should have to feel the pain that the students of Marjory Stoneman Douglas High School and their families felt. As a Broward County resident, our hearts are broken but our resolve is as strong as ever. Many communities across the country have also experienced that heartbreak and loss firsthand. The epidemic of gun violence has claimed far too many souls. I've heard it time and time again: there isn't a single law that can prevent all these tragedies from happening. And they're right. It's going to take an all-out, sustained, comprehensive effort to reduce gun violence and keep guns out of the wrong hands. You may have seen them on TV or through videos on the Internet. The students and the family members of Marjory Stoneman Douglas High School are taking action. They're demanding change because they understand that no generation should have to endure what they've been through. They won't be silenced. They won't be discouraged. And they're changing the conversation surrounding gun safety in America. [_**I 'm proud to lend them my voice and my support. I'm asking you to do the same so we can make our schools, our streets, and our states safer.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113f9c/622b7b5b/19d5a20f/307b8f42/1414447849/VEsH/ "http://demand-change-on-gun-safety.com/#dws") | | | [Add Your Voice](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113f9c/622b7b5b/19d5a20f/307b8f42/1414447849/VEsE/ "http://demand-change-on-gun-safety.com/#dws")
---
Thank you,
Debbie

|

| | | **Announcing ... Team Donnelly's Record Breaking One-Day Goal!
Goal: $10,000
Deadline: MIDNIGHT
[Pitch in $10 >>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c23fa/7377f838/3109f2dd/51faf05f/841001959/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-onedaygoal319?refcode=20180319.1nd&amount=10")**

Peter,

I know I don't have to tell you how much is at stake in the 2018 election. Grassroots donors like you have stepped up in a big way to fuel recent Democratic victories in districts that the political pundits said we could _never_ compete in. I don't know about you, but those wins in places like Alabama and Pennsylvania fired me up!

**Now, we 've been challenged to break our own online fundraising record. In 2012, after Richard Mourdock's now infamous debate, Team Donnelly's grassroots donors raised more than $8,500 in less than 24 hours! I know it'll be tough, but I think if everyone chips in today, we can break this record. Will you help us out?**

Breaking this one-day record will prove to Mitch McConnell and the Koch Brothers that Indiana's Senate seat isn't for sale to the highest bidder. It will prove that our team is ready for the fight in November -- and, most importantly, it will send a message that Democrats can compete, and win, _anywhere_ in 2018.

We have to re-elect Joe, and it won't happen unless we build a campaign that can reach voters in each of Indiana's 92 counties. We're not leaving any county behind. **If there 's been one lesson we've learned this year, it's that we have to compete everywhere.**

Peter, the Koch brothers and Mitch McConnell's allies are already working overtime to smear Joe's record on TV. I'm ready to show them the power of our team and smash this record to pieces.

**If you 're with me, then I need you to chip in $10 before midnight tonight. [Please don't wait -- join us now.](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c23fa/7377f838/3109f2dd/51faf05f/841001959/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-onedaygoal319?refcode=20180319.1nd&amount=10")**
\-- Kelly

We've already hit 10k grassroots donors -- onwards and upwards! | [](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc49ae0/c1f7015/69a02e45/1143c957/778594365/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tina-smith?utm_source=ema_2018-03-19_mid-month-thanks-nondonor&refcode=ema_2018-03-19_mid-month-thanks-nondonor&utm_campaign=mid-month-thanks-nondonor&utm_medium=ema") Friend, It means so much to me to get to say this: Our campaign set a goal for 10k grassroots donors by Tuesday, 3/20, to show the Republicans how strong we are before the public end-of-quarter filing deadline. Not only did we hit 10k -- we blew that goal out of the water. We've reached 12,150 donors as of this morning. Thanks so much for supporting me, for having my back through this whole process. I know I can count on you while I'm working hard in the Senate. When I started in the Senate, I promised to be a fierce advocate for ALL Minnesotans. I'm thrilled to have hit our goal of 10k donors already because to me, it's a clear sign that you and I agree: listening to folks matters more than any personal agenda. [**It's not too late to be a part of this -- thousands of your friends and neighbors are stepping up and now I need you to do the same. The “yogurt mogul” Van Ostern will put our First-in-the-Nation Primary at considerable risk if elected New Hampshire Secretary of State. In New Hampshire, we take defending our FITN Primary seriously - but the Democrats seem pretty willing to disregard our traditions. How do we stop this failed Democrat gubernatorial nominee from taking control of all New Hampshire elections? subject: This is our last chance (net neutrality)
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MARTIN HEINRICH FOR SENATE
body:
|

| | | Friend,

Months ago, President Trump's Federal Communications Commission (FCC) chief ended the free and open internet as we know it.

I've joined the effort to **pass legislation to save net neutrality** , but **[we're still fighting for support.](http://nm.martinheinrich. "https://petitions.signforgood.com/save_net_neutrality/?code=heinrich&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fmth-net-neutrality-ja%3Frefcode%3DMH_EM_PT_180DO_180319_1_1%26amount%3D27%26recurring%3Dtrue?firstName=&lastName=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=87103&city=Albuquerque&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=heinrich&utm_content=1+-+wersquore+still+fighting+for+support&utm_campaign=MH_EM_PT_180DO_180319_1_1&source=MH_EM_PT_180DO_180319_1_1&refcode=MH_EM_PT_180DO_180319_1_1")**

Guaranteeing a level playing field online is critical for allowing New Mexico students and businesses to compete, and it is vital we protect open access to information.

[**This is our LAST chance to save net neutrality. That 's why I need to hear if you stand with me. Will you join me and urge the rest of my Senate colleagues to save net neutrality?**](http://nm.martinheinrich. "https://petitions.signforgood.com/save_net_neutrality/?code=heinrich&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fmth-net-neutrality-ja%3Frefcode%3DMH_EM_PT_180DO_180319_1_1%26amount%3D27%26recurring%3Dtrue?firstName=&lastName=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=87103&city=Albuquerque&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=heinrich&utm_content=2+-+This+is+our+LAST+chance+to+save+net+neut&utm_campaign=MH_EM_PT_180DO_180319_1_1&source=MH_EM_PT_180DO_180319_1_1&refcode=MH_EM_PT_180DO_180319_1_1")

If we find a balanced legislative solution to this issue, it will ensure Americans have the right to a free and open internet.

But if we don't, Americans will be subject to whatever the FCC chief decides each term.

That's not consistent. It isn't fair to businesses and students. And the Senate has a responsibility to act.

[**That 's why I'm standing up for net neutrality and our LAST chance to save it. Add your name here to show you support a free and open internet ->**](http://nm.martinheinrich. "https://petitions.signforgood.com/save_net_neutrality/?code=heinrich&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fmth-net-neutrality-ja%3Frefcode%3DMH_EM_PT_180DO_180319_1_1%26amount%3D27%26recurring%3Dtrue?firstName=&lastName=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=87103&city=Albuquerque&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=heinrich&utm_content=3+-+Thatrsquos+why+Irsquom+standing+up+for+n&utm_campaign=MH_EM_PT_180DO_180319_1_1&source=MH_EM_PT_180DO_180319_1_1&refcode=MH_EM_PT_180DO_180319_1_1")

I will make sure to keep you updated on this latest legislative fight,

Martin

Less than one month after President Trump tweeted his endorsement of right-wing extremist Lou Barletta for Pennsylvania's Senate seat, NBC News released their assessment of the 2018 Senate landscape and named PA a race to watch.
We have to get serious about protecting this seat, because it's going to be an uphill battle. Bob Casey is a fighter for middle-class families, for workers, and for seniors, but his campaign is falling behind.
Ever since Senator Casey voted against the GOP's tax scheme, which gives the majority of its benefits to corporations and the 1%, Republicans have stepped up their attacks against him. Team: The #NationalWalkoutDay students are leading the charge for gun reform in our country -- but they're also being viciously derided by far-right Republicans here in Florida.

[**We 're collecting names to show that Florida Democrats are standing with these students ahead of Saturday's March For Our Lives. Sign on to show your support.**](http://act.floridadems. Friend --

The **derision and ridicule aimed at the students** standing up to the NRA and pushing for real gun reforms must be condemned -- especially when **they 're coming from the NRA spokesperson and even some elected Florida Republicans.**

[**Student voices are leading the movement for gun reform, and we need to stand by their side and denounce every attempt by far-right extremists to silence them. SIGN YOUR NAME to stand with the #NationalWalkoutDay students!**](http://act.floridadems. These students are our future.

The cold and callous remarks against them show that **we have got to vote these Republicans out.**

When NRA-backed politicians and spokespeople are lashing out at children for standing up for what's right, you know it's time for them to go.

They're not representing their constituents or America's values. They represent the gun lobby and only the gun lobby.

[**SIGN ON to join us in standing shoulder to shoulder with the students calling for action. Add your name to denounce those who are trying to silence the student protests!**](http://act.floridadems. subject: Supreme Court BLOCKS gerrymandering
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tom Wolf for Governor
body:
\-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | [**BREAKING NEWS: "Supreme Court Rejects GOP Plea To Keep Gerrymandered House Map In Pennsylvania"[1]**](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecfb6/73a2bc12/3fe3cac9/1fccf488/3023910160/VEsH/ "https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/s/ty-wolf-gerrymander-scotus?utm_campaign=em18_180319_scotus_clk_clq_act_ger_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=wolf&source=em18_180319_scotus_clk_clq_act_ger_&utm_content=1+-+BREAKING+NEWS+Supreme+Court+Rejects+GOP+")

Thanks to Democratic Governor Tom Wolf vetoing Republicans' gerrymandered map, Pennsylvania now has fair maps!

**Sign Immediately:[Thank Governor Wolf for ending gerrymandering in Pennsylvania ->](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecfb6/73a2bc12/3fe3cac9/1fccf48f/3023910160/VEsE/ "https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/s/ty-wolf-gerrymander-scotus?utm_campaign=em18_180319_scotus_clk_clq_act_ger_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=wolf&source=em18_180319_scotus_clk_clq_act_ger_&utm_content=2+-+Thank+Governor+Wolf+for+ending+gerrymand")**
* * *
This is huge news:

Just last month, Governor Wolf rejected Republicans' gerrymandered maps in Pennsylvania. So new and fair maps were drawn to replace the old maps.

Republicans were determined to keep their old, gerrymandered map in place -- so they took their case to the U.S. Supreme Court.

_But the Supreme Court just rejected their plea. That means Pennsylvania will keep fair maps in place!_

**Sign Immediately:[Thank Governor Wolf for ending gerrymandering in Pennsylvania and setting a model for the nation ->](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecfb6/73a2bc12/3fe3cac9/1fccf48e/3023910160/VEsF/ "https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/s/ty-wolf-gerrymander-scotus?utm_campaign=em18_180319_scotus_clk_clq_act_ger_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=wolf&source=em18_180319_scotus_clk_clq_act_ger_&utm_content=3+-+Thank+Governor+Wolf+for+ending+gerrymand")**

This is the step forward our country needs to end gerrymandering nationwide.

Across the country, Republicans have been drawing districts in their favor -- to please their big donors and special interests. They think they can do whatever they want without facing consequences from voters.

Look, if we're going to put an end to gerrymandering, we need Democratic governors like Tom Wolf who will take on Republicans and fight for fair maps.

**[Please, take a moment to thank Governor Wolf for ending gerrymandering in Pennsylvania and setting a model for the nation:](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecfb6/73a2bc12/3fe3cac9/1fccf48d/3023910160/VEsC/ "https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/s/ty-wolf-gerrymander-scotus?utm_campaign=em18_180319_scotus_clk_clq_act_ger_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=wolf&source=em18_180319_scotus_clk_clq_act_ger_&utm_content=4+-+Please+take+a+moment+to+thank+Governor+W")**

**[https://secure.wolfforpa.com/Pennsylvania-Gerrymandering](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecfb6/73a2bc12/3fe3cac9/1fccf48c/3023910160/VEsD/ "https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/s/ty-wolf-gerrymander-scotus?utm_campaign=em18_180319_scotus_clk_clq_act_ger_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=wolf&source=em18_180319_scotus_clk_clq_act_ger_&utm_content=5+-+httpssecurewolfforpacomPennsylvaniaGerry")**

Thanks,

_Wolf for PA_

[1]. Huffington Post. _Supreme Court Rejects GOP Plea To Keep Gerrymandered House Map In Pennsylvania._[03/19/18](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecfb6/73a2bc12/3fe3cac9/1fccf4b3/3023910160/VEsA/ "https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/s/ty-wolf-gerrymander-scotus?utm_campaign=em18_180319_scotus_clk_clq_act_ger_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=wolf&source=em18_180319_scotus_clk_clq_act_ger_&utm_content=6+-+031918")
